_comment: ADAM governance config. Profiles SELECT a policy_bounds ruleset; rulesets DEFINE what is allowed. Rules live in policy_bounds only -- never duplicated onto a profile -- so there is one source of truth. Slice 1: this data model exists and is attached to sessions, but NOTHING here is enforced yet. Enforcement arrives in Slice 2 (skill availability) and Slice 3 (the post-synthesis policy gate). Edit this file to add profiles or rulesets; it is read at session start.

standard.name: Standard
standard.description: General-purpose bounds. All skills available. This is the default and is intentionally permissive so that enabling governance changes no existing behavior; per-role denials (e.g. pilots' email block) still apply on top via the role layer. Choose education_safe or strict for constrained sessions.

education_safe.name: Education Safe (FERPA/COPPA-aware)
education_safe.description: K-12 bounds. Artifacts allowed; external sends blocked; student-data and public-facing output gated for review.

strict.name: Strict (external actions restricted)
strict.description: Most restrictive. Any external action, email, or file write requires explicit approval.

general.name: General
general.description: Default profile. No human review; standard bounds.

education.name: Education
education.description: K-12 deployments. Human review for public-facing artifacts; FERPA/COPPA-aware bounds.

data_intelligence._comment: Agent Data Intelligence scope (read-only). Seeker/Truthseeker may query the listed ratified sources; aggregate-only by default; denied_fields are always unreachable (enforced at the pipeline Sentinel, not the planner). Budgets bound queries per deliberation.

external_action_restricted.name: External Action Restricted
external_action_restricted.description: Human review required before any external action; strict bounds.
